summary: All three persistent SAST findings (CWE-502, CWE-94, CWE-78) remain completely unaddressed after three attempts. The code directly uses pickle.loads on untrusted input without safe-type validation, eval on user-supplied script without sandboxing, and subprocess with shell=True using unsanitized f-string input, allowing arbitrary code and command injection.
suggested_actions: Replace pickle.loads with a safe deserialization mechanism (e.g., JSON) or implement a restricted unpickler that validates types before deserialization.
Replace eval with ast.literal_eval or a properly sandboxed execution environment with restricted builtins.
Replace shell=True subprocess with a shell-less subprocess call using a list of arguments, and avoid constructing shell commands from untrusted input.
Add input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data before use in any dangerous function.

evidence.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
evidence.tool_snippet:
13     form = FieldStorage(fp=request.rfile, headers=request.headers)
14     config = pickle.loads(form.getvalue('config'))
15     script = form.getvalue('script')
evidence.context:
11:     
    12:     # Parse multipart form data
    13:     form = FieldStorage(fp=request.rfile, headers=request.headers)
>>  14:     config = pickle.loads(form.getvalue('config'))
    15:     script = form.getvalue('script')
    16:     
    17:     # Validate and deserialize configuration file
